title: lathyrus digitatus var. stenophyllus
intro: Lathyrus digitatus var. stenophyllus, also known as narrow-leaved peacock lathyrus or narrow-leaved peacock's ear, is a plant species within the genus Lathyrus, commonly found in Asia. The plant contains a toxic compound called ODAP, which can be toxic to humans if ingested in large amounts, but it also has medicinal properties due to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Lathyrus digitatus var. stenophyllus include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, fever, and skin conditions, as well as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a medical professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
